criteria.definition: The revision preserves every controlled fact, date, quantity, condition, and required action without adding unsupported claims.
The revision makes the situation and required action unambiguous to a reader encountering the notice without additional context.
The revision uses vocabulary, tone, and explanatory detail appropriate for the audience named in the controlled contract.
The revision removes avoidable words and repetition without omitting facts, qualifications, or instructions needed by the audience.

admissibility.eligible: Every required behavior and hard constraint is satisfied.
admissibility.ineligible: At least one required behavior or hard constraint is violated.
admissibility.unknown: At least one requirement cannot be determined from supplied artifacts and none is proven violated.

outcome_rule.both_eligible: Apply Pareto comparison across material criterion decisions; criteria are applicable and scored only in this state.
outcome_rule.criteria_not_applicable: Use null criteria whenever either candidate is ineligible or unknown; the mechanical outcome uses eligibility only.

evidence.rule: Evidence must cite a bounded one-based inclusive line range in reconstructed candidate bytes or a controlled contract field, quote exact lexical bytes from that range, provide the exact typed requirement/status or criterion/winner semantic anchor, repeat both quote and semantic anchor in the observation, and explain the implication; labels, verdicts, unrelated prose, and unbounded line claims are not evidence.
